pre_registered_predictions.P1_gamma1_null: PASS: gamma=1 has no conservative sampled excess and no terrain feedback in all scenarios.
pre_registered_predictions.P2_noise_guard: PASS: normal and storage do not pass after subtracting the conservative margin, including at gamma=0.
pre_registered_predictions.P3_main: PASS: stress_context at gamma=0 survives sampled CHSH margin and changes next-phase P_release by +19.998558%.
pre_registered_predictions.P4_threshold: PASS: stress_context gamma=0.25 survives weakly (+1.438596%), while gamma>=0.5 does not.

safe_interpretation: A finite-shot sampled CHSH measurement-boundary signal, after subtracting a conservative Bell-bound margin, can still be written into terrain and later modulate classical-effective dynamics in stress context. This is a model-level positive for sampled measurement-boundary terrain feedback, not for ordinary local population plumbing.
limitations: Not chemical realism or biological metabolism.
The result still uses simulated quantum states and simulated finite-shot CHSH measurements.
The positive effect depends on a CHSH/noncommutative readout and terrain feedback component.
Local population plumbing without measurement-boundary writeback remains reduced-Arm2 reproducible.
